title: Infinite loop of asynchronous statistics loading
description:
## Bug Report

Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks!

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)

1. Start the cluster with a mock patch:
```diff
diff --git a/pkg/statistics/handle/syncload/stats_syncload.go b/pkg/statistics/handle/syncload/stats_syncload.go
index 4463d1d03a..85693dd2d4 100644
--- a/pkg/statistics/handle/syncload/stats_syncload.go
+++ b/pkg/statistics/handle/syncload/stats_syncload.go
@@ -437,6 +437,8 @@ var errGetHistMeta = errors.New("fail to get hist meta")
 
 // readStatsForOneItem reads hist for one column/index, TODO load data via kv-get asynchronously
 func (*statsSyncLoad) readStatsForOneItem(sctx sessionctx.Context, item model.TableItemID, w *statsWrapper, isPkIsHandle bool, fullLoad bool) (*statsWrapper, error) {
+       // mock timeout
+       time.Sleep(1 * time.Second)
        failpoint.Inject("mockReadStatsForOnePanic", nil)
        failpoint.Inject("mockReadStatsForOneFail", func(val failpoint.Value) {
                if val.(bool) {
```
2. Create a table and insert some data:
```sql
CREATE TABLE t1 (a INT, b INT, c INT, INDEX idx_b (b));
INSERT INTO t1 VALUES (1,3,0), (2,2,0), (3,2,0);
ANALYZE TABLE t1 ALL COLUMNS;
```
3. Run the query:
```sql
SELECT * FROM t1 where b = 2;
DROP TABLE t1;
```
4. Check the logs

### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)
Do not try to load the stats for the dropped table.

### 3. What did you see instead (Required)
It keeps trying it and prints a lot of logs:

```log
[2025/03/25 15:45:11.781 +08:00] [INFO] [read.go:798] ["Table statistics item not found, possibly due to table being dropped"] [category=stats] [tableID=80] [indexID=2]
```
